title: salix × fragilis var. rubens
intro: Salix × fragilis var. rubens, also known as the weeping willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a variety of the crack willow (Salix fragilis). The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Salix × fragilis var. rubens include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, as well as supporting digestive and respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions when used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
